mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and enhance DevOps practices. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher and Kubernetes clusters from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.2/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher-darwin-amd64 /usr/local/bin/rancher

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/rancher

```

> Download the Rancher CLI for macOS.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Make the CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server

sudo systemctl start rke2-server

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml

```

> Install RKE2 on the server.
> Enable the RKE2 server service.
> Start the RKE2 server.
> Set the kubeconfig environment variable.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the newly created cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Rancher Server
steps.description:
This step deploys the Rancher server on the Kubernetes cluster to manage it.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rancher/rancher/releases/download/v2.14.2/rancher.yaml

kubectl -n cattle-system rollout status deploy/rancher

```

> Deploy Rancher using the provided YAML.
> Check the rollout status of the Rancher deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher Server
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rancher server from the cluster. Note that this will result in data loss for any managed clusters.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/rancher/rancher/releases/download/v2.14.2/rancher.yaml

kubectl delete namespace cattle-system

```

> Delete the Rancher deployment.
> Remove the namespace used by Rancher.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher Server
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, backup your data. This step will upgrade Rancher to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n cattle-system -o yaml > rancher-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rancher/rancher/releases/download/v2.14.2/rancher.yaml

kubectl -n cattle-system rollout status deploy/rancher

```

> Backup the current Rancher deployment.
> Apply the new Rancher version.
> Verify the upgrade was successful.
commands: kubectl get all -n cattle-system -o yaml > rancher-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the Kubernetes API server's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the kubeconfig file is correctly configured and points to the right server.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "rancher/rancher:v2.14.2": rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: manifest for rancher/rancher:v2.14.2 not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image version does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the available tags for the Rancher image and ensure the correct version is specified.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the RKE2 server is not running or the kubeconfig is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the RKE2 server is running and the KUBECONFIG environment variable is set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Rancher server is not reachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the Rancher server is not running or there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Rancher deployment and ensure the necessary ports are open.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved RBAC features, and enhanced UI performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10 or earlier, ensure to review the new RBAC configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible Kubernetes version and sufficient resources before proceeding.
